ev.headline: ION Storage Systems Hits Major Solid-State Battery Milestone
ev.summary: Maryland-based ION Storage Systems achieved the first US solid-state battery cell qualification, with production expected to begin in 2026. The company's strategy of commercializing applications beyond EVs mirrors how lithium-ion batteries first entered the market.

ev.headline: PG&E to Deploy 3,800 Electric Fleet Vehicles by 2030
ev.summary: California's largest utility plans to expand its EV fleet and charging infrastructure, with 821 chargers currently installed and plans for 3,800 chargers across 88 sites by 2030. The initiative supports cost savings and renewable energy integration.

ev.headline: Global EV Charging Stations Projected to Reach 9 Million by 2026
ev.summary: Global public EV charging infrastructure is expected to surpass 9.01 million stations by year-end, with China leading expansion at 6.1 million stations while US momentum slows due to policy changes. Europe continues steady growth with expected additions of 219,000 stations.

ev.headline: Scout Motors Reports 87% of Reservations Favor Extended-Range Hybrid Over Full EV
ev.summary: Scout Motors' reservation data shows a significant shift toward extended-range hybrids, with 87% of 160,000 reservations favoring gas-powered options over full EVs. The trend reflects declining EV demand following elimination of federal tax credits.

ev.headline: BYD Sales Plunge 36% as Competition Intensifies in China's EV Market
ev.summary: BYD's combined January-February sales dipped 36% year-over-year as Chinese competitors including Leapmotor, Xiaomi, and Nio gain market share. The EV giant is launching new battery technology and flash charging to regain momentum.

ev.headline: 43 New EV Models Expected to Launch or Reach US Dealers in 2026
ev.summary: Despite slower EV adoption and reduced government incentives, automakers are pressing ahead with 43 electric vehicle launches planned for the US market this year, including affordable models like the Chevy Bolt and premium offerings like BMW and Mercedes models.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 Launch Could Be One of Fastest EV Ramps in US History
ev.summary: Rivian expects to sell 20,000-25,000 R2 SUVs in 2026 with first deliveries in June, potentially matching the Chevy Equinox EV's eight-month ramp to 20,000 units. The company has simplified manufacturing to achieve faster scale-up.

ev.headline: US EV Sales Remain Flat as Market Adjusts to Post-Incentive Environment
ev.summary: February 2026 US auto sales projected at 1.19 million units with BEV share at 5.6%, indicating flat EV growth as automakers and buyers adjust to the end of federal tax credits and evolving regulatory policies.

ev.headline: Global EV Market Entered 2026 with 'Unrecognizable' Landscape
ev.summary: Global EV sales declined 3% in January 2026, with China down 20% due to new purchase taxes. Europe showed growth at 24% year-over-year, while North America fell 33%, marking a pivotal shift in regional EV dynamics.

ev.headline: Major Automakers Write Off $55 Billion Over EV Strategy Resets
ev.summary: Stellantis announced $26.2 billion in write-downs, joining GM ($6B), Ford ($19.5B), and others in massive charges after overestimating EV adoption. Legacy automakers are pivoting toward profitable hybrids and delaying EV launches.

ev.headline: Kia Discontinues Niro EV as Company Focuses on Dedicated EV Lineup
ev.summary: Kia launched the upgraded Niro in Korea exclusively as a hybrid, discontinuing the Niro EV model. The company is concentrating on its new dedicated electric vehicles like the EV3, EV4, and EV5.

ev.headline: Chinese EV Maker Claims World's First Semi-Solid-State Battery with 620-Mile Range
ev.summary: A Chinese EV manufacturer unveiled a semi-solid-state battery with 500+ Wh/kg energy density, 30% higher than current lithium-ion. The technology is expected to enter mass production by end of 2026.

ev.headline: Solid-State Battery Hits 80% Charge in 4.5 Minutes in Independent Test
ev.summary: Independent testing by Finland's VTT confirmed Donut Lab's solid-state battery can reach 80% charge in under 5 minutes, though industry skepticism remains about commercial viability and production timelines.

ev.headline: China Develops Lithium Metal Battery Exceeding 700 Wh/kg Energy Density
ev.summary: Chinese scientists published breakthrough battery research in Nature journal, achieving over 700 Wh/kg energy density at room temperature. EVs equipped with the new batteries could exceed 1,000 km range and enter mass production by end of 2026.

ev.headline: V2G Market Projected at £129.8 Billion by 2034 as Vehicle-to-Grid Technology Scales
ev.summary: Vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology is accelerating globally, with the market valued at approximately £11.4 billion in 2024 projected to reach £129.8 billion by 2034. Major automakers including Tesla, GM, and BMW are scaling V2G-capable vehicle production throughout 2026.

ev.headline: EV Realism Emerges as Automakers Face Reality of Slower Adoption
ev.summary: The automotive industry has shifted from EV euphoria to pragmatism as consumer demand fell short of expectations. Automakers are refocusing on gas vehicles and hybrids after billions in wasted capital.

ev.headline: Automakers' EV Strategy Reset to Impact 2026 US Production Trends
ev.summary: US automakers' EV strategy reset is expected to dampen production in 2026-2027, but recovery is projected by 2028-2029 as manufacturers maximize capacity with hybrids and ICE vehicles alongside selective EV models.

ev.headline: States Drive EV Transition Forward Despite Federal Policy Rollbacks
ev.summary: All 50 US states have adopted multiple EV policies including charging infrastructure incentives and purchase rebates, demonstrating continued commitment to electric vehicles despite federal policy changes.

energy.headline: California appeals court upholds Net Metering 3.0 rooftop solar program
energy.summary: A California appeals court upheld the state's less-generous net metering compensation rules, striking down legal challenges from solar advocates despite concerns about reduced incentives for solar adoption.

energy.headline: Home battery capacity surged 51% in 2025 as residential storage rapidly grows
energy.summary: Residential battery systems in the U.S. increased by 51% in 2025, with projections for 120% growth by 2030 as homeowners pair storage with rooftop solar and support grid stability.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves and energy prices soar
energy.summary: As net metering policies change and electricity prices rise, homeowners increasingly view battery storage as essential for energy independence rather than relying solely on exporting solar power to the grid.

energy.headline: Record 86 GW of U.S. capacity planned for 2026, dominated by solar and battery storage
energy.summary: Solar and battery storage will account for 79% of 86 GW of new utility-scale capacity coming online in 2026, marking the largest annual increase in over two decades with solar generation expected to grow to over 420 TWh.

energy.headline: 24.3 GW of new battery storage projected for 2026, surpassing 2025 record
energy.summary: The EIA expects 24.3 GW of new battery energy storage to come online in 2026, continuing the momentum that saw batteries transition from fast-growing addition to foundational grid asset.

energy.headline: Solar installers shift to leases as federal tax credits end for purchased systems
energy.summary: With the 30% federal tax credit ending for purchased rooftop solar in 2026, installers are steering customers toward leased systems that remain eligible for incentives through 2027 and beyond.

energy.headline: Home electrification market surged 205% before tax credit deadline
energy.summary: U.S. homeowners rushed to install solar before the 30% federal tax credit ended December 31, 2025, with a 205% surge in installations and adoption of whole-home energy solutions combining solar, storage, and EV charging.

energy.headline: AI-powered battery management and vehicle-to-home technology reshape residential storage in 2026
energy.summary: Advanced battery management systems and V2H technologies are making home energy storage more intelligent and integrated, with electric vehicles becoming part of whole-home power solutions.

energy.headline: New York offers stacked solar incentives: 25% state tax credit plus federal 30% credit in 2026
energy.summary: New York homeowners can take advantage of a 25% state solar tax credit up to $5,000, combined with the 30% federal credit, plus net metering compensation for excess energy production.

energy.headline: Virtual power plants and home batteries proven critical for grid stability in 2025
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes in virtual power plant demonstrations dispatched 535 megawatts of electricity, demonstrating that residential solar and storage systems can meet significant grid demand.

energy.headline: States revamp net metering policies and community solar programs in Q1 2025
energy.summary: 47 states plus D.C. took distributed solar policy action in early 2025, with trends including iterative net metering revisions, community solar overhauls, and expanded system size limits.

energy.headline: Rooftop solar booming but missing underserved communities despite accessibility gains
energy.summary: While U.S. solar adoption has become the fastest-growing power source with over 5 million installations, high upfront costs and policy misalignment continue to exclude lower-income households from residential solar benefits.

energy.headline: Texas dominates 2026 solar boom with 40% of planned utility-scale installations
energy.summary: Texas will host approximately 40% of the nation's planned solar construction in 2026, with 17.4 GW of utility-scale solar expected to come online as the solar boom continues.

energy.headline: Solar panels remain worth it in 2026 despite end of 30% federal tax credit
energy.summary: SolarReviews analysis shows residential solar installations are still financially viable in 2026 despite the end of the 30% tax credit, with rising electricity costs and favorable net metering policies providing strong returns.

energy.headline: Lithium-ion battery chemistries dominate 2026 energy storage outlook amid safety focus
energy.summary: While lithium-ion remains the dominant battery chemistry for 2026, pressure is building for longer-duration storage, safer alternative chemistries, and more resilient domestic supply chains.

energy.headline: U.S. domestic battery manufacturing faces oversupply concerns despite record demand
energy.summary: Despite strong demand for energy storage, U.S. battery manufacturers announced capacity that may exceed domestic demand by 2026, raising questions about factory utilization and future competitiveness.

energy.headline: Sacramento Municipal Utility District planning 160 MW battery storage facility on decommissioned nuclear site
energy.summary: SMUD is advancing a major 640 MWh battery energy storage system project to be built on a former nuclear power plant site, launching construction in June 2026.

energy.headline: Electricity costs rising 39% as AI data centers increase demand, pushing solar value higher
energy.summary: Average U.S. utility rates increased 39% from November 2020 to November 2025, adding over $630 annually to household electricity costs and making rooftop solar more economically attractive.

energy.headline: Los Angeles City Council commits to 50% renewable energy production by 2030
energy.summary: The Los Angeles City Council passed a motion directing the city to achieve 50% renewable energy generation by 2030, boosting the region's clean energy goals.

energy.headline: Home energy storage moves from niche to mainstream as technology and financing improve
energy.summary: Home battery storage is evolving into a central component of modern energy-independent homes, with advances in battery technology, smart controls, and flexible financing models driving 2026 adoption.

energy.headline: Pennsylvania net metering remains favorable for residential solar investment in 2026
energy.summary: Pennsylvania homeowners can still benefit from fair net metering policies that credit solar exports, making solar financially attractive even after the end of the 30% federal tax credit.
